$comment: Modeled after CEP 24 - Specification of environment.yml input files (https://conda.org/learn/ceps/cep-0024/).
title: Conda environment
description: A conda environment definition file (environment.yml / environment.yaml), as consumed by `conda env create` and produced by `conda env export`.

name.description:
Preferred name of the environment. Tools may let the user override it.

Must not be `base` or `root`, and must not contain `/`, spaces, `:` or `#`.

channels.description: Conda channels queried by the solver, given as names, URLs or local paths. The configured default channels are appended unless `nodefaults` is present.

items.description: A channel name (e.g. `conda-forge`), URL or local path. Use `nodefaults` to drop the configured default channels.

dependencies.description: Packages to install. Plain strings are conda MatchSpec requirements; mapping entries are subsections handled by other installers (e.g. `pip`), processed after the conda requirements.

anyOf.description: A conda MatchSpec requirement, e.g. `python`, `numpy>=1.21`, `python=3.11.*` or `conda-forge::numpy=1.21.*`.
A non-conda installer subsection (keyed by the installer name).

pip.description: PyPI requirements installed with pip after the conda packages.

items.description: A PyPI requirement specifier, e.g. `requests`, `flask==3.0.*` or `-r requirements.txt`.

variables.description: Environment variables associated with the environment. Stored in `conda-meta/state` and retained by `conda env export`.

additionalProperties.description: Variable value. Non-string values are converted to strings.

prefix.description: Preferred absolute path to the environment. Often ignored by tools. Tildes and environment variables are expanded.

platforms.description: Target platforms (conda subdirs) the environment should solve for. Used mainly by lockfile tools. `noarch` is not a valid value here.

category.description: Free-form annotation of the kind of dependencies in this file (e.g. `main`, `dev`, `test`). Used mainly by lockfile tools.
